title: brachyloma elusum
intro: Brachyloma elusum, also known as the "false tick vine," is a type of flowering plant in the family Epacridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, and its leaves and stems are valued for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with Brachyloma elusum include relief from skin irritations, wounds, and infections, as well as the potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
